commit.message:
Fix ATmega328P RAM overflow: PROGMEM controls and streaming INFO_CONTROLS

Move controlDefs, control name/description strings, and enum labels to
PROGMEM. Replace 450-byte stack buffer with streaming sendInfoControls()
using pgm_read_byte/pgm_read_word/pgm_read_ptr for all PROGMEM access.
RAM usage drops from overflow to 52% (1079 bytes), leaving 969 for stack.

commit.message:
fix monitoring targets: replace blocked/removed URLs with machine-readable endpoints

- SSA: swap CDN-blocked HTML pages for RSS feed, JSON stat endpoints, DKAN
  catalog API, and sitemap.xml to avoid Akamai bot-blocking on GitHub Actions
- ACL: replace two genuinely removed pages (NIDILRR, NCI) with NARIC grantee
  database and ACL programs directory as sentinels
- WhiteHouse: replace removed accessibility page with GSA Section508.gov
- Census: deduplicate targets, add ACS B18101 API (state-level JSON data),
  upgrade variable list to 2023, add SIPP row count check
- CDC: point BRFSS/NHIS to annual data file indexes instead of topic pages;
  update SODA query to searchContext parameter
- ED: add CKAN API endpoint and CRDC downloadable data files page
- BLS: add data tables page and flat-file download server
- CMS: replace HTML search with data.json catalog and dual-eligible summary
- data.gov: replace HTML search with CKAN JSON API
- ACL: add National Core Indicators data portal (nationalcoreindicators.org)
- NIH: replace POST-only Reporter endpoint with API explorer + NIDCD stats page
- GSA: add Section 508 program and agency reporting pages

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Fix candle intro: move script inline so it works before CDN loads

On slow mobile connections, CDN scripts (GSAP/Lenis) block page load for
several seconds. The intro was visible but tapping did nothing because the
event listener in script.js hadn't attached yet. Moving the intro IIFE to
an inline script right after the intro HTML means it runs immediately when
that HTML is parsed, with zero CDN dependency. Also increased auto-skip
timer from 3s → 7s.

commit.message:
feat: add spread value logging for stop formula backtesting

- Add SV: {call}/{put} suffix to heartbeat entry detail lines in MEIC base
  strategy, enabling HOMER to parse per-entry spread values from logs
- Add spread_snapshots table to backtesting DB (schema v3) with timestamp,
  entry_number, call_spread_value, put_spread_value
- Add parse_spread_snapshots() and parse_all_spread_snapshots() to HOMER
  data_collector for extracting SV data from log files
- Integrate spread snapshot collection into _populate_db_for_date() and
  backfill pipeline

This enables future stop formula optimization by recording the actual
cost-to-close (spread value) over time for each entry, which was previously
unavailable for backtesting analysis.

Add CTRL_TYPE_ENUM support across firmware, protocol parser, and UI

Implements Phase 2 of the enum controls proposal: firmware declares
discrete controls (like local_mode) as CTRL_TYPE_ENUM with named
options, which flow through the binary/JSON protocol to render as
dropdown selects instead of sliders or number inputs.

commit.message:
v0.6.2: Major architecture refactoring and security hardening

- Refactored monolithic web_server.py (1200+ lines) into modular architecture:
  - app/routes/: Blueprint-based route organization (quests, crafting, config, data)
  - app/services/: Business logic (cache_service, character_service)
  - app/utils/: Shared utilities (security, validation, responses, constants)
  - app/factory.py: Flask application factory pattern

- Security improvements:
  - Path traversal prevention with safe_read_json() and validate_path()
  - Input validation schemas for all user inputs
  - Restrictive CORS configuration (localhost only)
  - Security headers (CSP, X-Frame-Options, X-Content-Type-Options)
  - Log data sanitization to prevent sensitive data exposure
  - Stack trace suppression in production mode
  - Secure file permissions on logs/config (0600)

- Performance enhancements:
  - Thread-safe caching service with TTL and file modification tracking
  - Rate limiting middleware (120 req/min, 20 burst)

- API standardization:
  - Consistent response format: {success, data, meta, error}
  - Proper HTTP status codes and error codes
  - Centralized response utilities

- Code quality:
  - Eliminated magic numbers via constants.py
  - Extracted duplicate code to utilities
  - Type hints and docstrings throughout
  - Original monolithic code preserved in web_server_legacy.py

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>

Rebuild UI for modern standards + migrate to Vercel

commit.message:
Enable GOP cache for multi-viewer support

Late-joining viewers need the cached keyframe + AVC sequence header
to start decoding. With gop_cache: false, second viewer gets black
screen because mpegts.js can't initialize without initial codec info.

commit.message:
feat: Comprehensive product audit — 35+ improvements across 8 areas

Authentication & Security:
- Add password reset flow (forgot password → email → reset page)
- Fix account deletion to also remove auth.users entry
- AdminGuard re-checks role every 5 minutes (handles revocation)

Credit System & Rate Limiting:
- Fix guest daily reset bug (counter now resets at UTC midnight)
- Add daily credit replenishment for free users (3/day, cap 20)
- Unified credit display for all tiers (guest/free/pro)
- Redis rate-limit fallback (permissive on failure)
- Auto-refund for abnormally short stream responses

Core Enhancement Flow:
- Genius questions now update after refinement (not same questions)
- Preserve original input across refinement iterations
- Streaming cursor blink animation during generation
- Stream interruption detection with retry UI
- Request deduplication via AbortController
- Copy without watermark for Pro users

Library & Favorites:
- Auto-categorize prompts on save from enhancement category
- Duplicate detection with warning toast
- Enhanced search (title + content + tags + use_case)

test(m2): add M2→M3 event contract tests (ExposureEvent, MetricEvent, QoEEvent) (#112)

40 integration tests validating the M2→M3 data path:
- 12 ExposureEvent contract tests (roundtrip, interleaving provenance map,
  lifecycle segment enum, assignment probability, Delta schema alignment)
- 10 MetricEvent contract tests (roundtrip, properties map, zero/negative
  values, content_id for interference, session_id for session-level)
- 10 QoEEvent contract tests (roundtrip, PlaybackMetrics all fields,
  CDN/ABR/encoding, Delta schema alignment, QoE field coverage)
- 8 Kafka integration tests (produce/consume roundtrip, headers+key
  verification, partition determinism, cross-topic user correlation)

This unblocks Agent-3 (metric computation) and Agent-4 M4b (reward events).

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Add DeepSeek-V3 embedding dimension comparison (§9)

Three-way cosine comparison: random S^7167, 128×S^55 concatenated,
and real DeepSeek-V3 input embeddings. Shows that multi-head
structure doesn't exist in input embeddings (both random models
identical), but real embeddings are 2x wider with learned structure.
Precomputed histogram data is 4KB.

commit.message:
fix: make Meta OAuth resilient for testers without IG Business pages

fetchInstagramAccount was fatal — if a tester had no Facebook page with
an Instagram Business account linked, the entire OAuth callback returned
502 and the token was never saved. Now it's non-fatal (like fetchAdAccount),
the token is always persisted, and the user can fill in Account ID manually.

Also adds missing OAuth scopes (instagram_content_publish,
instagram_manage_comments, pages_read_engagement, pages_manage_posts)
and exposes has_token/needs_manual_config in the API response.

- refactor: card implementations (OpponentBomb, OpponentFlagErase, OpponentFlagReshuffle, Smoke, Trebuchet) for state system compatibility
- fix: JsonUtils.Settings exposed as public static for shared serialization configuration
- remove: legacy GrainState.cs in favor of new State abstraction
- add: StatesSetup.cs for centralized state configuration registration

commit.message:
fix: move _orgs block to end of registry in seed script

The _orgs block was being added incrementally inside the loop, causing
subsequent org sections to appear after it. Since restore/sync scripts
break at _orgs:, this meant non-first orgs were never processed.
Now strips any existing _orgs block before seeding and appends it
once at the very end with all orgs deduplicated.
